data.title: Wipro partners with Odido for AI-enabled end-to-end IT modernization
data.description:
• Wipro (WIT) has inked a multi-year agreement with Odido Netherlands to transform its IT landscape and enhance customer experience across their enterprise and consumer segments.
  • By combining AI and deep consulting expertise, Wipro will help Odido improve customer engagement and satisfaction, improve productivity, and streamline operations to reduce costs, the company said.
  • As part of the engagement, Wipro will lead a full-scale modernization of Odido’s digital and enterprise technology landscape as well as drive IT simplification and automation.
  • This transformation will be powered by Wipro’s WEGA and WINGS AI delivery platforms, part of Wipro Intelligence™.
  • WIT +1.12% after hours to $2.72.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bearish sentiment edges lower, shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Bearish sentiment among individual investors eased to 42.7% for the week ending November 26, down from 43.6% a week earlier, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment edged slightly lower to 32.0%, compared with 32.6% in the previous week.

Global markets showed a generally positive tone this week, with Asia and Europe posting gains in several sessions. Asia stocks were trading higher while tracking Wall Street’s tech recovery, and Europe markets moving into the green on hopes of a U.S. or December Fed rate cut. China markets were supported by stimulus hopes, even as some property-sector names came under pressure.

Still, sentiment was mixed in places. Australia’s inflation came in hotter than expected, Germany recorded no growth in Q3, and worries around AI valuations, crypto moves and earlier bouts of volatility appeared in the headlines. At the same time, U.S. markets still saw periods of strength, with Wall Street finishing higher on fresh economic signals ahead of Thanksgiving and stocks rising as investors assessed additional data.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also rose during the week, with the figure standing at 25.3%, compared to 23.9% last week. 

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Medical Facilities renews normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Medical Facilities (DR:CA) (OTCPK:MFCSF) has received approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange to make a normal course issuer bid for up to 1,805,324 of its 18,053,349 outstanding common shares, representing 10% of its public float.
  • The corporation may purchase the shares at prevailing market prices during the period from December 1, 2025, to November 30, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Corsair Gaming insider Thi La snaps up 50,000 shares
data.description:
• Corsair Gaming (CRSR) shares rose 7.5% in the after-hours trading on Wednesday following an insider purchase by CEO Thi La, who acquired 50,000 shares for approximately $305,000 on November 25, 2025, at $6.10 per share.
  • Thi La, CEO of Corsair Gaming's subsidiary and a 10% owner, now holds 480,726 shares after the buy, with no sales from her in the past year.
  • This comes on the heels of a leadership transition, with Gordon Mattingly set to become CFO, succeeding Michael G. Potter, effective December 2, 2025.

data.title: EZGO Technologies files $200M mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• EZGO Technologies (EZGO) has filed a registration statement with the SEC using a “shelf registration” process to offer up to US$200M in mixed securities.
  • The company may issue any combination of ordinary shares, debt securities, warrants, rights, or units in one or more offerings.

data.title: Protagenic Therapeutics receives Nasdaq non-compliance letter
data.description:
Protagenic Therapeutics (PTIX) has received a notification letter from Nasdaq indicating its non-compliance with the exchange’s listing rules, namely the minimum stockholders' equity requirement and timely filing of periodic reports.

The exchange told the company it no longer meets the required $2.5 million minimum stockholders’ equity based on its latest Form 10-QT. The exchange also said the company is out of compliance for not yet filing its September 30, 2025 Form 10-Q. The company says it is finishing the remaining work and expects to file the delayed report soon.

Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5815, because the company recently regained compliance and remains subject to a mandatory panel monitoring period, the Nasdaq Listing Qualifications Staff has issued a determination to delist the company's securities.

The company will request a hearing before a Nasdaq panel to present its plan to address both deficiencies.

Source: Press Release
